REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR MAKRAS, VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CC 1942.4 IS GRANTED WITH PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF'S DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE FILED A DAY AFTER THE DUE DATE FOR THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF, IS SET ASIDE. CRAVENS V. STATE BD. OF EQUALIZATION (1997) 52 CAL.APP.4TH 253; MARY MORGAN, INC. V. MELZARK (1996) 49 CAL.APP.4TH 765. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR MAKRAS AND VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND CONTINUED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO VICTOR MAKRAS AND VICTOR G. MARKAS, INC. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT "LANDLORD" AS USED IN CC 1942.4 IS DEFINED BY SFRO AND NOT BY CC 1940.8.5. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE NO EVIDENCE AND NO LEGAL THEORY UPON WHICH MAKRAS MAY BE HELD LIABLE AS ALTER EGO OF ANNA PAPOULIAS, AN INDIVIDUAL. MOTION IS CONTINUED ON THE ISSUE OF CC 1942.4 (2) TO OCTOBER 13, 2022, THE LAST DAY TO HEAR A MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION UNDER CCP 437C. NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 27, 2022 PLAINTIFF TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF (NOT TO EXCEED TWO PAGES) AND ADMISSIBLE COMPETENT EVIDENCE REGARDING CC 1942.4(2). NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 4, 2022 DEFENDANT TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY BRIEF (NOT TO EXCEED 2 PAGES) AND OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, IF ANY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR MAKRAS, VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED. Summary Adjudication as to the cause of action for violation of CC 1942.4 is granted with prejudice. Plaintiff's dismissal without prejudice filed a day after the due date for the supplemental brief, is set aside. Cravens v. State Bd. of Equalization (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 253; Mary Morgan, Inc. v. Melzark (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 765. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Continued To Oct-19-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501

DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is continued to October 19, 2022 on Court's own motion.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-15-2022 Continued To Oct-13-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTS ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR MAKRAS AND VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and CONTINUED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Victor Makras and Victor G. Markas, Inc. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that "landlord" as used in CC 1942.4 is defined by SFRO and not by CC 1940.8.5. Furthermore, Plaintiff fails to provide no evidence and no legal theory upon which Makras may be held liable as alter ego of Anna Papoulias, an individual.
Motion is continued on the issue of CC 1942.4 (2) to October 13, 2022, the last day to hear a motion for summary adjudication under CCP 437c. No later than September 27, 2022 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed two pages) and admissible competent evidence regarding CC 1942.4(2). No later than October 4, 2022 Defendant to file a supplemental reply brief (not to exceed 2 pages) and objections to evidence, if any. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
